# Plan

## Overview

Accelerate Picky Products to first £ of affiliate revenue by sequencing work in revenue-impact order: fix monetisation hygiene, fill the content queue with the first five Candidates, automate the remaining manual steps, add visual differentiation and a feedback loop, then process the remaining five Candidates informed by early data. All changes target solo operation under 30 minutes per week.

## Scope

- In: affiliate link audit; batch-processing 10 Candidates in two staged tranches; auto-image download from Amazon CDN; pin template improvements including ASA `#ad` compliance; Pinterest analytics feedback loop via API; Pinterest SEO enrichment in descriptions; PA API readiness script; weekly digest script
- Out: paid tools, paid advertising, new channels (Pinterest is primary until 50+ distributed pins), changes to the Make webhook or Pinterest OAuth flow, structural Notion schema changes beyond adding fields

---

## Phases

### Phase 1: Revenue Foundation

**Goal**: Ensure every live and queued pin links correctly and fill the first content tranche before any other work. Both tasks require no new infrastructure.

#### Task 1.1: Affiliate link hygiene audit

- Location: Notion Products DB (`a2c18096-68b1-8219-bb44-01b7fa5c9611`), Distribution DB (`c7718096-68b1-83ea-8ab2-01b6e3a2b2fe`)
- Description: Verify `tag=pickyprod-21` is present in the final destination URL for all products (3 distributed, 2 active, 10 Candidates). Confirm the Distribution DB `Affiliate Link` rollup is non-null for all Scheduled/Distributed records. Fix any gaps in Products DB. This is the highest-leverage change — a broken tag means zero commission on every click.
- Estimated Tokens: 3,000
- Dependencies: None
- Steps:
  - Query Products DB; extract `Affiliate Link` for all records
  - For each `amzn.to` link, follow redirect and confirm `tag=pickyprod-21` in final URL
  - For any missing tag, patch Products DB with full `amazon.co.uk/dp/ASIN?tag=pickyprod-21` URL
  - Query Distribution DB for all Scheduled/Distributed records; confirm `Affiliate Link` rollup is populated (non-null)
  - Add a note to the `/process-product` skill: always use full `amazon.co.uk` URL with tag, never bare `amzn.to` without verified tag
- Acceptance Criteria:
  - All 15 products have `tag=pickyprod-21` in their resolved affiliate URL
  - Zero Scheduled/Distributed Distribution DB records return null for `Affiliate Link` rollup

#### Task 1.2: Process first 5 Candidate products

- Location: `pins/`, Notion Products DB and Distribution DB
- Description: Process the five Candidates with highest review count and best fit for the three supported personas — these are most likely to generate early clicks. Order: Manta Sleep Mask (~14k reviews), Brown Noise Machine (~11k), Dreamegg D11 (~3.6k), LectroFan (~2.2k), Good Nite Weighted Blanket. Run `/process-product` then `/generate-pins` for each, using rolling start dates continuing from the current last scheduled pin.
- Estimated Tokens: 60,000 (5 × ~12,000)
- Dependencies: Task 1.1 complete (links verified before new records created)
- Steps:
  - Run a single test product end-to-end before batching to catch any skill bugs
  - Query Distribution DB for the latest `Publish Date` across all active records — this is the start date for product 1
  - For each product: run `/process-product <name> --start-date <date>`, then drop `product.jpg` from Amazon listing, then run `/generate-pins <name>`
  - Verify `Enrichment Status = Scheduled` in Notion and GitHub Pages images resolve (HTTP 200) before moving to the next product
  - Confirm `publish_due_pins.py` dry-run returns the next 7 pins in correct date order after the final product
- Acceptance Criteria:
  - 5 products at `Enrichment Status = Scheduled`; 45 Distribution DB records created with valid `Publish Date`, `Pin Title`, `Affiliate Link`
  - 45 pin images live at `https://mjorquera.github.io/picky-products/pins/<slug>/`
  - No publish-date collisions: no two Distribution DB records share the same `Publish Date`

---

### Phase 2: Automation and Visual Quality

**Goal**: Eliminate the manual image-save step and improve pin visual differentiation before the second Candidate tranche is processed.

#### Task 2.1: Auto-download product image from Amazon CDN

- Location: New `fetch_product_image.py`; integrated into `/process-product` skill
- Description: Products DB stores `Amazon Main Image URL` (e.g. `https://m.media-amazon.com/images/I/71eiAJ99OgL._AC_UL320_.jpg`). Replace `_AC_UL320_` with `_AC_SL1500_` to request the full-resolution image. Download and save as `pins/<slug>/product.jpg` at `/process-product` time — not at `/generate-pins` time — to avoid mid-batch failures from expired CDN URLs.
- Estimated Tokens: 3,000
- Dependencies: Task 1.2 complete (validates `Amazon Main Image URL` field is consistently populated)
- Steps:
  - Write `fetch_product_image.py <slug>`: reads `Amazon Main Image URL` from `hooks.json`, substitutes `_AC_UL320_` → `_AC_SL1500_`, fetches with `requests.get` and a `Mozilla/5.0` User-Agent, saves to `pins/<slug>/product.jpg`
  - Verify fetched image is ≥600px on short edge via `Image.open().size`; abort with clear error if smaller or if CDN returns non-200
  - Integrate call into `/process-product` skill immediately after writing `hooks.json`; skip gracefully if `product.jpg` already exists (≥500KB)
  - Add `amazon_image_url` key to the `hooks.json` schema written by `/process-product`
  - Update CLAUDE.md to remove manual image-save step from content workflow
- Acceptance Criteria:
  - Running `/process-product <name>` on a new Candidate produces `pins/<slug>/product.jpg` without manual intervention
  - Fetched image is ≥600px short edge
  - Graceful error (not stack trace) if CDN returns 403/404, with fallback instruction to drop `product.jpg` manually

#### Task 2.2: Add persona-tinted template and ASA compliance

- Location: `generate_pins.py`
- Description: Two changes in one pass. First, add Template C: a subtle persona-tinted background (Hot Sleeper `#E8F4FD` cool blue, Anxious/Insomniac `#F0EEF8` warm lavender, Light Sleeper `#E8EEF5` pale slate) replacing the current Template A (clean label) — same filename convention, no Distribution DB changes. Second, add `#ad` in 9pt at the bottom-right of the pill on all templates to satisfy ASA requirements. Optionally add a `£XX on Amazon` sub-line in the pill if `price` is set in `hooks.json`.
- Estimated Tokens: 6,000
- Dependencies: Task 2.1 (validates image pipeline is stable before modifying the generator)
- Steps:
  - Define `PERSONA_TINTS = {"Hot Sleeper": (232,244,253), "Light Sleeper": (232,238,245), "Anxious/Insomniac": (240,238,248)}` in `generate_pins.py`
  - Add `render_template_c(product_img, hook_text, angle)`: fill canvas with tint, paste product image centred at 60% height, render hook text without pill
  - Replace Template A output with Template C; keep Templates B (hook-a) and B-variant (hook-b) unchanged
  - Add `#ad` label (9pt, 50% opacity dark text) to bottom-right of pill in all templates
  - Add `price` to `hooks.json` schema in `/process-product`; if populated, render `£XX on Amazon` as secondary pill line
  - Verify contrast ratio ≥4.5:1 programmatically before committing
  - Limit initial rollout to newly processed products; do not regenerate already-scheduled pins (Pinterest caches image URLs at pin-creation time)
- Acceptance Criteria:
  - Three angle variants are visually distinct from each other and from the prior all-white output
  - `#ad` label present on every generated pin
  - Pill height adjusts for two-line price variant without clipping
  - Template B (hook variants) logic is unmodified

---

### Phase 3: Analytics Feedback Loop

**Goal**: Create a lightweight weekly signal on which hooks, angles, and products are generating outbound clicks — automated, no new paid tools.

#### Task 3.1: One-time Pinterest account health baseline

- Location: Pinterest Business Analytics (web UI) — 10 minutes, manual
- Description: Before investing in visual work, confirm pins are being distributed. Check board-level impressions and profile reach for the past 30 days. If impressions are near zero, the issue is account age or thin profile — not visual quality — and Phase 2 effort should be deprioritised. Document the baseline in Notion.
- Estimated Tokens: 500
- Dependencies: None
- Steps:
  - Open Pinterest Business Analytics → Overview → select UK Comfort Products for Sleep board → note impressions, saves, outbound clicks for last 30 days
  - Create a Notion page: "Pinterest Baseline [date]" with these three numbers
  - If board impressions < 500 in the last 30 days, flag this as a distribution problem rather than a creative problem
- Acceptance Criteria:
  - Baseline numbers documented in Notion before Task 2.2 work begins
  - Go/no-go decision on visual overhaul recorded

#### Task 3.2: Add Pinterest Pin ID capture and analytics sync

- Location: `publish_due_pins.py`, new `analytics_sync.py`, Notion Distribution DB
- Description: Add three fields to Distribution DB — `Pinterest Pin ID` (text), `Impressions` (number), `Outbound Clicks` (number). Modify `publish_due_pins.py` to parse the Pin ID from the Make webhook response and write it back to Notion. Write `analytics_sync.py` to call Pinterest Analytics API weekly per published pin and update both numeric fields.
- Estimated Tokens: 8,000
- Dependencies: Task 3.1 (account health confirmed before building analytics)
- Steps:
  - Add `Pinterest Pin ID`, `Impressions`, `Outbound Clicks` fields to Distribution DB via `mcp__notion__API-update-a-data-source`
  - Test one manual Make run to inspect the webhook response format and confirm Pin ID is present; if not, use Pinterest Analytics API date-range query to match by title as fallback
  - Modify `publish_due_pins.py`: after successful Make call, parse Pin ID from response and PATCH the Notion record
  - Write `analytics_sync.py`: query all Distribution DB records with non-null `Pinterest Pin ID` and `Status = Scheduled`; call `GET /v5/pins/{pin_id}/analytics?metric_types=IMPRESSION,OUTBOUND_CLICK` for trailing 30 days; PATCH Notion records. Run weekly via Cowork cron.
  - Add `Performance` formula field to Distribution DB: `Outbound Clicks / Impressions * 100`
- Acceptance Criteria:
  - `publish_due_pins.py` writes non-null `Pinterest Pin ID` to Notion after a live publish
  - `analytics_sync.py` runs without error and populates fields for at least one pin with >48h of data
  - Script handles missing/revoked pins gracefully (logs warning, skips, continues)
  - Weekly cron added alongside existing daily publisher

---

### Phase 4: Scale and Pinterest SEO Deepening

**Goal**: Process the remaining 5 Candidates informed by 2–3 weeks of early analytics data, and improve discoverability of all published pins with keyword enrichment.

#### Task 4.1: Pinterest SEO enrichment in `/process-product`

- Location: `/process-product` skill prompt
- Description: Update the skill to include 2–3 long-tail UK search terms in every pin description (e.g. "best cooling pillow UK", "pillow for hot sleepers UK review", "sleep accessories UK"). No code change — prompt update only. This is near-zero effort with meaningful long-term discoverability impact on Pinterest's search index.
- Estimated Tokens: 1,000
- Dependencies: None — can be applied before processing the second tranche
- Steps:
  - Update `/process-product` skill prompt: add instruction to include 2–3 UK-specific long-tail sleep product search terms in each pin description, formatted naturally (not keyword-stuffed)
  - Verify output on one test product before batch processing
- Acceptance Criteria:
  - New Distribution DB records contain at least one long-tail UK search term per pin description (e.g. contains "UK")
  - Terms are embedded naturally, not appended as a raw keyword list

#### Task 4.2: Process remaining 5 Candidate products

- Location: `pins/`, Notion Products DB and Distribution DB
- Description: Process the remaining 5 Candidates — Memory Foam Neck Support Pillow, Sacred Thread Bamboo Sheet Set, Bambaw Bamboo Fitted Sheet, LINENWALAS Bedding Set, Cooling Pillow Shredded Memory Foam — using the same rolling start-date approach. By this point auto-image download (Task 2.1), the updated template (Task 2.2), and the SEO-enriched skill (Task 4.1) are all in place, so these products benefit from all improvements.
- Estimated Tokens: 60,000 (5 × ~12,000)
- Dependencies: Tasks 2.1, 2.2, 4.1 complete; at least 2 weeks of analytics data available from Phase 1 products to validate or adjust approach
- Steps:
  - Review `analytics_sync.py` output: if any angle or hook type shows zero outbound clicks across Phase 1 products, adjust `/process-product` skill defaults before this batch
  - Process each product sequentially; verify `Enrichment Status = Scheduled` and GitHub Pages live before moving to the next
- Acceptance Criteria:
  - All 10 Candidates at `Enrichment Status = Scheduled`
  - 90 total new Distribution DB records with no publish-date collisions
  - Publishing queue extends at least 90 days from today

---

### Phase 5: Operational Efficiency and PA API Readiness

**Goal**: Reduce weekly manual work to under 15 minutes and prepare for PA API activation.

#### Task 5.1: Weekly digest script

- Location: New `weekly_digest.py`
- Description: A terminal-printable weekly health check. Prints: pins published in the last 7 days, upcoming 7 scheduled pins, any records where `Publish Date < today` and `Published Link` is null (failed publishes), and top 5 by `Outbound Clicks` once analytics are populated.
- Estimated Tokens: 2,000
- Dependencies: Task 3.2
- Steps:
  - Write `weekly_digest.py`: queries Distribution DB for last 7 days, next 7 days, and failed-publish flags; prints structured stdout summary in under 5 seconds
  - Add to CLAUDE.md session-open checklist: "run `python3 weekly_digest.py` first"
- Acceptance Criteria:
  - Script completes in under 5 seconds
  - Failed publish detection correctly identifies `Publish Date < today` + null `Published Link`
  - Renders cleanly when analytics fields are null (no division by zero)

#### Task 5.2: PA API readiness script

- Location: New `pa_api_check.py`, `.env`
- Description: When the 3 qualifying sales milestone is reached, activation is not automatic. This script reduces integration time to under 15 minutes once credentials are granted. It also validates that the tag and partner configuration are correct.
- Estimated Tokens: 2,000
- Dependencies: Task 1.1 (affiliate links verified clean)
- Steps:
  - Add `PAAPI_ACCESS_KEY`, `PAAPI_SECRET_KEY`, `PAAPI_PARTNER_TAG` as empty placeholders with comments to `.env`
  - Write `pa_api_check.py`: calls `GetItems` for one known ASIN; prints title, price, availability; exits with human-readable error (not stack trace) if credentials are empty
  - Document in CLAUDE.md: "run `python3 pa_api_check.py` after adding credentials"
- Acceptance Criteria:
  - Script exists with no hardcoded credentials
  - Clear human-readable error if `.env` keys are empty
  - No additional packages required beyond `requests` (already installed)

---

## Testing Strategy

- **Task 1.1**: Follow each affiliate link with `curl -sIL <url> | grep -i location`; confirm `tag=pickyprod-21` appears in final redirect URL for all 15 products.
- **Task 1.2**: After full batch, run `publish_due_pins.py` in dry-run mode; confirm next 7 pins return valid image URLs and non-null affiliate links. Verify no two records share a `Publish Date`.
- **Task 2.1**: Delete `product.jpg` from a test slug; run `/process-product`; confirm file appears at `pins/<slug>/product.jpg` with short edge ≥600px (`Image.open().size`). Test graceful failure with a broken URL.
- **Task 2.2**: Eyeball-test all three tinted variants alongside existing Templates B. Programmatically verify contrast ratio ≥4.5:1. Confirm `#ad` present on every output. Test empty `price` field renders without error.
- **Task 3.2**: Publish one live pin; confirm `Pinterest Pin ID` written to Notion within 60 seconds. Run `analytics_sync.py` next day and confirm `Impressions` field populated for at least one pin.
- **Task 4.1**: Spot-check three new Description fields from the second batch and confirm at least one UK search term present per description.
- **Regression**: After each phase, run `publish_due_pins.py` dry-run to confirm pipeline is intact.

---

## Risks

- **Amazon CDN URL expiry**: `_AC_SL1500_` URLs for product images are generally stable but can rotate. Mitigation: `fetch_product_image.py` validates file size >10KB after download; prints a clear fallback instruction if CDN returns non-200. Downloading at `/process-product` time (not `/generate-pins`) reduces the window for expiry between write and use.
- **Make webhook may not return Pinterest Pin ID**: If the Make Pinterest module does not surface the created Pin ID in its response, `publish_due_pins.py` write-back will silently fail. Mitigation: test with one manual Make run before coding; if unavailable, fall back to Pinterest Analytics API date-range query matching by title to recover Pin IDs.
- **Template C may underperform white backgrounds**: Pinterest's algorithm behaviour varies by category; no A/B evidence exists. Mitigation: limit Template C to the second Candidate tranche initially; compare `Outbound Clicks` via `analytics_sync.py` before rolling back or forward. Do not regenerate already-scheduled pins (Pinterest caches image URLs at creation time — regeneration is ineffective for live pins).
- **10 Candidates queued without performance data**: Filling 90 days of queue with unvalidated content risks poor product-type fit. Mitigation: staged approach — process 5 first (Phase 1), validate with analytics, then process 5 more (Phase 4) with adjustments if needed.
- **Pinterest account distribution problem**: If the account is penalised for being new or thin, visual improvements have no effect. Mitigation: Task 3.1 baseline check gates Phase 2 visual work. If impressions are near zero, investigate account health before investing further.
- **PA API 180-day application window**: Amazon Associates UK closes accounts without 3 qualifying sales within 180 days. Confirm application date; if fewer than 60 days remain, prioritise higher-price products (Weighted Blanket £65, LectroFan £70) in the publishing queue to maximise conversion value per click.

---

## Rollback Plan

- **Task 1.1 (link patches)**: All Products DB changes recoverable from Notion page history. Revert via Notion UI if a link was overwritten incorrectly.
- **Task 1.2 (batch processing)**: Distribution DB records created after the batch start date can be set to `in_trash: true` via Notion MCP. GitHub Pages images removed by deleting `docs/pins/<slug>/` and committing. Products DB `Enrichment Status` reset to `Candidate` manually.
- **Task 2.1 (image auto-download)**: If a fetched image is lower quality than expected, `git checkout HEAD -- pins/<slug>/product.jpg` restores a manually-placed version. Guard: script skips fetch if `product.jpg` already exists and is ≥500KB.
- **Task 2.2 (template change)**: `git revert` on `generate_pins.py` restores prior templates. Re-run `/generate-pins` for affected not-yet-published products and push to GitHub Pages. Already-published pins are unaffected (Pinterest caches the image URL).
- **Task 3.2 (Notion schema)**: New Distribution DB fields can be removed via `update-a-data-source` with the property set to `null`. `publish_due_pins.py` changes revert via git.
- **Task 5.2 (PA API script)**: Additive file with no effect until credentials are added. Delete `pa_api_check.py` and remove `.env` placeholder keys if unwanted.

---

## Edge Cases

- **Candidate with no `Amazon Main Image URL`**: `fetch_product_image.py` must check for null before fetching; log a clear warning and fall through to manual-drop instruction rather than aborting the batch.
- **Publish-date collisions**: When processing multiple products, each product's start date must be the previous product's last `Publish Date` + 1 day. Query Distribution DB for `MAX(Publish Date)` before each `/process-product` call; do not use today's date.
- **Restless Sleeper angle**: CLAUDE.md specifies `Restless Sleeper` is not a valid Angle select option and the field must be omitted. Scan all new Distribution DB records for `Angle = Restless Sleeper` before the first publish run from the new batch.
- **`publish_due_pins.py` runs during GitHub Pages propagation**: If a pin image URL returns 404 when Make fetches it, the Pinterest publish fails silently. Add an HTTP HEAD check for the image URL before sending to the Make webhook; retry once after a 30-second delay.
- **Slug collision**: If two products normalise to the same directory name, `/process-product` may overwrite an existing folder. Add a collision check that appends `-2` if the directory already exists.
- **PA API activation mid-batch**: If PA API activates while Candidates are mid-process, do not mix `amzn.to` and PA API links within a single product's 9 pins. Process all pending products with the current link format first; switch link type at a clean product boundary.

---

## Open Questions

- What is the Amazon Associates UK application date? If fewer than 60 days remain in the 180-day activation window, reorder the Candidate queue to prioritise higher-price products.
- Does the Make Pinterest module return the created Pin ID in its webhook response? The answer determines whether Task 3.2 is a 2-hour task or requires a Pinterest API polling fallback.

## Comparative Analysis

Plan 1 is the strongest on risk analysis and self-critique — its suggestion to process 5 Candidates first, see data, then process the remaining 5 is the most defensible sequencing. It also catches the publish-date collision edge case explicitly. However it's verbose in places, and its Amazon image URL approach (strip modifier entirely) is less correct than Plan 2's `_AC_UL320_` → `_AC_SL1500_` substitution.

Plan 2 adds the most unique value on two points: ASA `#ad` compliance on every pin (a real legal obligation missed by Plans 1 and 3) and the insight that image download should happen at `/process-product` time, not `/generate-pins` time, to prevent half-processed batches. Its analytics approach (manual Notion log) is pragmatically sound but less scalable than API-based.

Plan 3 is the cleanest structurally and adds the only mention of Pinterest SEO (keyword enrichment in descriptions) — a near-zero-effort improvement missed by the others. Its PA API readiness script is sensible future-proofing. The dark gradient template is a bigger aesthetic gamble than persona-tinted backgrounds, with less rationale.

All three plans underspecify a one-time Pinterest account health baseline check, which should gate investment in visual work.

## Missing Steps
- One-time Pinterest account health check (board impressions, follower count) before committing to visual overhaul — surfacing problem invalidates all visual effort
- ASA `#ad` disclosure requirement on every pin (Plan 2 only)
- Pinterest SEO enrichment: keyword-dense descriptions, board name alignment with UK search terms (Plan 3 only, briefly)
- Single-product test run before full batch to catch skill bugs
- Image download at `/process-product` time, not `/generate-pins` time (Plan 2 only)
- PA API readiness script so activation-to-live is minutes not days (Plan 3 only)
- Correct Amazon image URL substitution: `_AC_UL320_` → `_AC_SL1500_` specifically (Plan 2 only)

## Contradictions
- Amazon image URL manipulation: Plan 1 strips the modifier entirely; Plan 2 correctly replaces with `_AC_SL1500_`; Plan 3 is unspecified. Use Plan 2's approach.
- Image download timing: Plan 1 says at `/generate-pins` time; Plan 2 argues `/process-product` time is more robust (avoids mid-batch failures). Plan 2 is correct.
- Batch size: Plans 2 and 3 process all 10 Candidates in one go; Plan 1 (self-critique) recommends 5 then 5 after data. Plan 1's staged approach is more defensible.
- Template C aesthetic: Plan 1 and 2 use persona-tinted light backgrounds; Plan 3 uses a dark gradient. No A/B evidence for either — persona tints are lower risk for the sleep niche.

## Improvements
- Stage Candidate batch: process 5 first, run for 2–3 weeks, use early analytics to validate product type fit before queuing the remaining 5
- Enforce ASA compliance (`#ad` in pill and pin description) from the start — retroactive fixes are harder
- Add keyword enrichment to `/process-product` skill: 2–3 long-tail UK sleep terms per description (e.g. "cooling pillow UK", "best pillow for hot sleepers UK")
- Capture Pinterest Pin ID via Make webhook response at publish time so analytics loop is possible without manual pin URL entry
- Baseline account health check before visual work to confirm pins are being distributed

# Plan

## Overview

Accelerate Picky Products to first-£ affiliate revenue by addressing the three highest-leverage levers in order: content volume (10 queued Candidates are idle), monetisation hygiene (some pins lack price signals; PA API needs 3 sales), and performance visibility (no feedback loop). Visual differentiation and channel analysis follow once publishing throughput is healthy.

## Scope

- In: processing the 10 Candidate products, automating product image retrieval, adding a Pinterest analytics poller, adding a third pin template (persona-tinted background), verifying affiliate link hygiene across all published pins, channel opportunity analysis
- Out: paid advertising, new platform build-out (Instagram, TikTok, SEO blog), changes to Make webhook configuration, structural changes to Notion schema

---

## Phases

### Phase 1: Content Velocity and Monetisation Hygiene

**Goal**: Clear the 10-product Candidate backlog and ensure every live pin links correctly — the two highest-impact actions that require no new infrastructure.

#### Task 1.1: Audit and fix affiliate link hygiene on all Published pins

- Location: Notion Distribution DB (`34618096-68b1-8227-ade9-0785f977dfae`)
- Description: Query all Published pins. For each, verify: (a) `Affiliate Link` rollup is populated (not null), (b) the `?tag=pickyprod-21` tag is present in the source Product DB entry, (c) `Published Link` field has a Pinterest URL where possible. Flag any Product DB entries where `Affiliate Link` is `amazon.co.uk/dp/...` without the tag. Patch them via Notion MCP. This is the most direct monetisation lever — every broken link is lost commission.
- Estimated Tokens: 8,000
- Dependencies: none
- Steps:
  - Query Distribution DB filtered to `Status = Published`
  - For each result, extract `Affiliate Link` rollup value
  - Cross-check parent Product DB entry's `Affiliate Link` field for `tag=pickyprod-21`
  - For any missing tag: update the Product DB record with the corrected URL
  - Log findings — how many pins were live without a valid tag
- Acceptance Criteria:

Stripping the size modifier (`._AC_UL320_`) gives full-resolution: `https://m.media-amazon.com/images/I/71eiAJ99OgL.jpg`. Write a short script that reads this URL from `hooks.json` (or directly from Notion via product slug), fetches the full-res image with `requests`, and saves it as `pins/<slug>/product.jpg`. This eliminates the single remaining manual step in the content workflow.

Task 1.1 — manually spot-check 5 Published pins by following the `Affiliate Link` URL directly; confirm it loads the correct Amazon product with the affiliate tag visible in the URL.

## Risks

- **Amazon image CDN URL manipulation may fail.** Stripping the size modifier from `_AC_UL320_` URLs is a widely-used pattern but is not formally documented by Amazon. CDN URLs can change, return 403 for non-browser requests, or serve a different image than the main listing photo. Mitigation: set a `User-Agent` header mimicking a browser in `fetch_product_image.py`, and add a fallback prompt instructing the user to drop `product.jpg` manually if the fetch returns a non-200 status.
- **Pinterest analytics coverage is incomplete.** Looking at the Distribution DB, `Published Link` is populated for only a subset of Published pins (e.g., Zello Light Sleeper pins have it; many Silentnight pins do not). The analytics poller will silently miss unpopulated pins, producing a biased sample. Decisions based on this data will reflect only the pins that happened to have their link recorded, not overall performance. Mitigation: add a count to the weekly report header ("Analytics based on X of Y published pins") so the coverage gap is always visible. Separately, add a step to Task 1.1 that attempts to backfill missing Published Links from Pinterest API using board content listing.
- **Self-critique: Phase 1.3 processes 10 products before any analytics exist.** The plan batches all 10 Candidates without knowing which product types (pillows vs sound machines vs bedding) perform best on Pinterest. Filling 90 days of queue with unvalidated content is volume-for-volume's-sake. If pillows underperform sound machines (plausible, given pillows are commoditised on Pinterest), 3 months of the queue will be suboptimal. The mitigation is weak — Phase 2 analytics only land after Phase 1 is done. Have you considered processing 5 Candidates first (the ones with strongest social proof scores), running analytics for 3 weeks, then processing the remaining 5 based on what's converting?
- **Self-critique: Template C's tinted background may not improve CTR and could harm it.** The plan assumes visual differentiation improves performance without evidence. Pinterest's algorithm can deprioritise non-white backgrounds in some categories, and the sleep niche leans toward clean, clinical aesthetics. The plan has no rollback mechanism at the pin level — once Template C images are generated and scheduled, they publish automatically. If Template C performs worse, the damage accumulates over weeks before the analytics loop catches it. Consider limiting Template C to 2 products initially and comparing performance before rolling it out across the full backlog.
- **PA API activation requires 3 qualifying purchases, not just clicks.** All phases drive traffic but none directly controls purchase conversion. The amzn.to short links already carry the affiliate tag. The risk is that Pinterest drives saves/impressions but UK buyers browse and purchase later without the cookie window (Amazon Associates: 24h). Higher-commission products (weighted blankets at £65, LectroFan at £70) should be prioritised in the publishing queue over lower-price items to maximise commission per purchase event.

Task 1.1 (affiliate link fixes) — if a Product DB affiliate link is updated incorrectly, the previous value is recoverable from Notion's page history. No automated rollback needed; revert manually via Notion UI.

Task 1.2 (auto image fetch) — if `fetch_product_image.py` overwrites a manually-placed high-quality `product.jpg` with a lower-quality fetched image, git history recovers it (`git checkout HEAD -- pins/<slug>/product.jpg`). Add a guard: skip fetch if `product.jpg` already exists and is ≥500KB.

Task 1.3 (bulk processing) — Distribution DB records created by `/process-product` can be deleted via Notion MCP in bulk if a product is processed incorrectly. Task 2.1 (analytics poller) — script is read-only against Pinterest API and write-only to a local CSV. No destructive operations. Stop running it by removing the cron entry or simply not running it.

Task 3.1 (Template C) — if Template C images look wrong after generation, `generate_pins.py` is re-run with the corrected template. The old images on GitHub Pages are overwritten by a new push. Scheduled Distribution DB records pointing to `pin-3-{angle}-hook-b.png` are unaffected as long as the filename is preserved.

## Edge Cases

- **Candidates with no `Amazon Main Image URL` field populated**: Task 1.2 must check for null before fetching. If null, log clearly and fall through to the manual-drop instruction. Looking at the data, the 4 early Candidates (Memory Foam Neck Support Pillow, LINENWALAS, Bambaw, Good Nite Weighted Blanket) do have `Amazon Main Image URL` populated with `_AC_UL320_` URLs, so the pattern should hold.
- **Publish date collisions when processing 10 products**: if `/process-product` is called for multiple products with overlapping start dates, two products will have pins scheduled on the same day. The daily publisher sends only one pin per run (based on `publish_at <= now`), so if two are due, only the first found is sent. Resolve by computing start dates programmatically in Task 1.3 to ensure strict non-overlap.
- **Restless Sleeper angle**: CLAUDE.md documents that `Restless Sleeper` is not a valid Angle select option and the field must be omitted. This is already handled in the workflow but worth verifying when processing the 10 Candidates — some product types (e.g., the Weighted Blanket) may naturally prompt a Restless Sleeper angle in copy generation. Ensure the `/process-product` skill enforces the 3-persona selection constraint.
- **PA API activation window**: Amazon Associates UK requires 3 qualifying sales within 180 days of application or the account is closed. If the account was opened recently, there is a deadline in play. Verify the application date and calculate how much runway remains. If <60 days remain, consider de-prioritising lower-priced products in the queue to maximise conversion value per click.
- **GitHub Pages image URL caching**: Pinterest may cache image URLs at pin-creation time. If a pin image is regenerated (e.g., Template C rollout), the old cached image may continue displaying on Pinterest even after the GitHub Pages file is updated. This means regenerating images for already-published pins is ineffective. Only apply Template C to not-yet-published pins.

## Risks
- **Amazon CDN image quality:** The `Amazon Main Image URL` in Notion stores `_AC_UL320_` thumbnail URLs (320px). At 1000×1500px canvas this will upscale badly. Mitigation: replace `_AC_UL320_` with `_AC_SL1500_` in the URL before downloading to request the full-res version; abort if the resulting image is under 600px on the short edge.
- **Pinterest pin publishing silently failing:** `publish_due_pins.py` updates Notion status to `Scheduled` after sending to Make, but does not confirm the pin is live on Pinterest. A Make scenario error would leave Notion showing `Scheduled` while the pin was never published. Mitigation: add a `Published Link` field check — if it is still null 24 hours after scheduled time, flag it in the weekly review.
- **10 Candidates at 1 pin/day = 90 days of runway**: at the current cadence the queue will exhaust in ~90 days with no new product discovery. Mitigation: the weekly routine (Task 5.1) includes a queue-depth check and triggers `/process-product` if fewer than 14 days remain.
- **Affiliate cookie attribution window:** Amazon Associates has a 24-hour add-to-cart window. Pinterest pins drive browse-intent traffic, not purchase-intent. Conversion rates will be low until there is enough volume (100+ distributed pins) to accumulate statistical signal. Mitigation: accept this as a structural constraint; focus on throughput (more products, more pins) over optimisation until 50 distributed pins exist.
- **Self-critique: The plan has no mechanism to detect if Pinterest is actually indexing or surfacing the pins.** There is no follower count check, no board performance baseline, and no keyword optimisation step. All visual improvements are produced in a vacuum — they could be improving pins that Pinterest is already distributing to zero people due to a new account penalty or thin profile. The plan should include a one-time check of the Pinterest Business account health (impressions on the board, follower count) before spending effort on visual work.
- **Self-critique: Task 1.2 (auto-download image) assumes Amazon CDN URLs are stable.** In practice, Amazon rotates some media CDN URLs. If a URL expires between Notion write and image download, the automation silently falls back to nothing. There is no fallback in the plan — the skill will error out mid-batch during Task 1.1 if a URL has expired, potentially leaving 3–4 products in a half-processed state. A more robust approach would be to download and commit the image at `/process-product` time, not at `/generate-pins` time.

- Tasks 1.1, 3.1, 4.2: Notion changes (new records, updated fields) can be reverted by querying the affected records and patching them back to prior values; Distribution DB records can be deleted individually via `mcp__notion__API-patch-page` with `in_trash: true`

- Task 5.2: documentation-only; no rollback needed

## Edge Cases
- A Candidate product has no `Amazon Main Image URL` in Notion: `/generate-pins` must detect the missing field and prompt for manual image placement rather than erroring silently

- `publish_due_pins.py` runs while a product is mid-generation (image files not yet on GitHub Pages): the Make webhook will receive an image URL that 404s. Guard: only include pins in the publish queue where `Image Link` is non-null in Notion
- The 10 Candidates are scheduled back-to-back with no gap: the `start_date` passed to each `/process-product` must be the day after the last scheduled pin date, not today's date — check the latest `Publish Date` in Distribution DB before each run

# Plan

## Overview

Improve Picky Products across five dimensions — visual quality, performance visibility, monetisation speed, pipeline throughput, and operational efficiency — with all changes scoped to solo, sub-30-minute-per-week operation. The plan is sequenced so the first two phases deliver revenue-path improvements before anything else, and every task is automatable with existing tools (Python, Claude Code, Notion MCP).

## Scope

- In: pin image template improvements; a lightweight Pinterest analytics feedback loop via Notion; batch processing of 10 waiting Candidates; PA API activation path; operational scripts to reduce manual steps
- Out: paid advertising, paid tools, abandoning Pinterest as primary channel, multi-person workflows, any channel requiring ongoing manual content creation (e.g. blog, TikTok)

---

## Phases

### Phase 1: Quick Wins — Revenue Path and Visibility

**Goal**: Unlock the first £ of affiliate revenue and create a feedback signal, both within one week of implementation. These are the two highest-leverage, lowest-effort changes.

#### Task 1.1: Batch-process all 10 Candidate products

- Location: `pins/`, `pins/*/hooks.json`, `pins/*/schedule_meta.json`, Notion Products DB, Notion Distribution DB
- Description: Run `/process-product` for each of the 10 Candidates in sequence, supplying a rolling start date so pins are queued immediately after the current Slumberdown schedule ends. This fills the publishing queue with ~90 days of content and ensures the channel stays active through the PA API activation window.
- Estimated Tokens: 30,000 (10 × ~3,000 per product run)
- Dependencies: None — Candidates already exist in Notion
- Steps:
  - Query Products DB for all records with `Enrichment Status = Candidate`; note product names in order of priority (prioritise by review count and price point within £20–£80 band)
  - Identify the last scheduled `Publish Date` across all active Distribution DB records — this becomes the `start_date` for the first new product
  - Run `/process-product <name>` for each product, passing start date; each subsequent product's start date = previous product's last pin date + 1 day
  - After each run, immediately drop `product.jpg` (from Amazon listing) into `pins/<slug>/` and run `/generate-pins <name>` — no batching delay
  - Verify Notion `Enrichment Status` = `Scheduled` for each product after `/generate-pins` completes
  - Verify `docs/pins/<slug>/` is pushed to GitHub Pages and images resolve in browser
- Acceptance Criteria:
  - All 10 Candidates have `Enrichment Status = Scheduled` in Notion
  - Distribution DB contains 90 new records (10 × 9) with valid `Publish Date`, `Pin Title`, and `Affiliate Link`
  - GitHub Pages hosts all images with HTTP 200
  - `publish_due_pins.py` dry-run confirms upcoming pins resolve correctly

#### Task 1.2: Add Pinterest performance columns to Distribution DB

- Location: Notion Distribution DB (`c7718096-68b1-83ea-8ab2-01b6e3a2b2fe`), `publish_due_pins.py`
- Description: Add three fields — `Pinterest Pin ID`, `Impressions`, `Outbound Clicks` — to Distribution DB so published pins can be tracked back to their record and performance data written back. This is the minimum viable feedback loop: no paid analytics tool required.
- Estimated Tokens: 2,000
- Dependencies: None
- Steps:
  - Add `Pinterest Pin ID` (text) field to Distribution DB via Notion UI or `update-a-data-source`
  - Add `Impressions` (number) and `Outbound Clicks` (number) fields
  - Modify `publish_due_pins.py`: after a successful Make webhook call, parse the Pinterest Pin ID from Make's response and write it back to the Notion record via `PATCH /pages/{page_id}` using `NOTION_TOKEN`
  - Write `analytics_sync.py`: script that reads all Distribution DB records where `Pinterest Pin ID` is set and `Status = Scheduled`, calls Pinterest Analytics API (`GET /pins/{pin_id}/analytics`), writes `Impressions` and `Outbound Clicks` back to Notion. Schedule via cron weekly.
- Acceptance Criteria:
  - `publish_due_pins.py` writes a non-null `Pinterest Pin ID` to Notion after a live publish
  - `analytics_sync.py` runs without error against one published pin and populates both numeric fields in Notion
  - Weekly cron entry added to Cowork (same mechanism as daily publisher)

**Goal**: Move pin templates from generic (white background, pill caption) to visually distinct, increasing click-through rate before the bulk of the new content goes live.

#### Task 2.1: Introduce a second pin template with lifestyle context

- Location: `generate_pins.py`, `pins/<slug>/`
- Description: Add `Template C` alongside existing A and B: a dark-gradient background (charcoal #1a1a2e to navy #16213e) with the product image inset and white headline text replacing the pill. This gives the grid two contrasting aesthetics rather than all-white, improves legibility for hook text, and costs zero extra effort per product because it replaces the existing Template A (clean label) which has the lowest creative value.
- Estimated Tokens: 5,000
- Dependencies: Task 1.1 (so new template applies to bulk queue)

  - Replace Template A (`pin-1-{angle}-clean.png`) output with Template C output — same filename convention, no hooks.json changes required

  - In `generate_pins.py`, add `draw_badge(canvas, label, position="top-right")` helper: draws a rounded rectangle in accent colour (`#e8c547` yellow) with dark label text

**Goal**: Maximise the probability that the 3 qualifying sales needed for PA API activation happen within the current publishing window, and ensure the system is ready to switch to PA API links the moment it activates.

- Description: All published pins use `amzn.to` short links. These work but lose the `tag=pickyprod-21` tracking if the redirect is followed and re-shared. **Goal**: Reduce the manual-work surface to under 15 minutes per week, leaving room for review.

## Risks

- **Make webhook response format may not include Pinterest Pin ID**: Make's standard Pinterest module may return a Pinterest API response body, but only if the module is configured to pass it through. If the Pin ID is not in the webhook response, Task 1.2's write-back will silently fail. Mitigation: test with one manual Make run and inspect the response before writing code; if unavailable, use Pinterest Analytics API with a date-range query to match pins by title.
- **Amazon CDN URLs in `Amazon Main Image URL` field are signed and expire**: Task 4.1 assumes the URL is stable. Amazon product image CDN URLs (`m.media-amazon.com`) are typically stable for product main images but can rotate. Mitigation: script must check file size after download and warn if < 10KB; fallback remains manual save.
- **10 Candidate products may have missing or incorrect Notion data**: Some Candidates may have no `Amazon Main Image URL`, no price, or a broken affiliate link — discovered during Task 1.1 batch run. Mitigation: add a pre-flight validation step at the start of `/process-product` that checks for required fields and halts with a human-readable error before writing any records.
- **Pinterest API rate limits on `analytics_sync.py`**: Pinterest Analytics API has per-app daily limits (currently 1,000 requests/day on Standard). At 1 request per pin, 90+ active pins will be fine, but will approach limits if run too frequently. Mitigation: weekly cron only; filter to pins published > 48 hours ago (fresh pins have no data anyway).
- **Self-critique: The plan assumes `/process-product` and `/generate-pins` skills exist as stable, tested code.** I have not been able to read the skill files directly to verify their exact behaviour. If either skill has bugs or undocumented edge cases (e.g. handling of products with no Angle assigned, or Candidates with partial data), the batch processing in Task 1.1 could create corrupted Distribution DB records. The plan should include a single-product test run before the full batch.
- **Self-critique: Task 2.1 recommends replacing Template A with a dark-gradient template without A/B data on which template performs better.** This is an assumption based on general Pinterest best-practice (contrast drives saves) but could be wrong for the UK sleep niche. If the dark template underperforms white, there is no rollback other than re-generating the images. A safer approach is to keep both templates and assign the dark variant to a subset of products — but this increases complexity and conflicts with the solo/low-effort constraint. The plan accepts the risk as a calculated bet rather than a data-driven decision.

---

## Rollback Plan

- **Task 1.1 (batch processing)**: If Distribution DB records are corrupt, query for all records created after the batch start date and set `Status = Draft`; re-run `/process-product` for affected products. GitHub Pages images can be re-pushed from local.
- **Task 2.1 (new template)**: Revert `generate_pins.py` to the previous version (git checkout); re-run `/generate-pins` for affected products to restore white-background images; push to GitHub Pages.
- **Task 1.2 (analytics fields)**: Notion fields can be deleted from the Distribution DB schema without affecting existing records. `publish_due_pins.py` changes are in git and can be reverted.
- **Task 4.1 (image download)**: If downloaded images are wrong, delete `product.jpg` from the slug folder and replace manually. Script does not modify Notion or push to git.
- **Task 3.2 (PA API script)**: File is additive and has no effect until credentials are added. Delete `pa_api_check.py` and remove `.env` placeholder keys if unwanted.

---

## Edge Cases

- A product has no `Amazon Main Image URL` in Notion: `download_product_image.py` must handle null URL gracefully — print a warning and skip rather than crash.
- A product's `Affiliate Link` is `amzn.to` format without a tag: Task 3.1 will catch this; the fix is to replace with a full `amazon.co.uk/dp/ASIN?tag=pickyprod-21` URL.
- Two products have the same or very similar names generating the same slug: `/process-product` or `generate_pins.py` may overwrite an existing folder. Add a slug-collision check at the start of both skills.
- `publish_due_pins.py` runs during a GitHub Pages propagation delay: if a pin image URL returns 404 when Make tries to fetch it, the Pinterest publish will fail silently. Mitigation: add a 30-second HTTP HEAD check for the image URL before sending to Make webhook; retry once.
- PA API activation triggers mid-batch: if PA API activates while 10 products are mid-process, the `Affiliate Link` source changes. Ensure all queued Distribution DB records use consistent links — do not mix `amzn.to` and PA API links within a single product's 9 pins.
- Pinterest `Restless Sleeper` angle: CLAUDE.md explicitly notes this is not a valid select option and the `Angle` field must be omitted. If any Candidate product's copy was drafted with a Restless Sleeper angle, `publish_due_pins.py` will attempt to set an invalid select and fail. Pre-flight: scan all new Distribution DB records for `Angle = Restless Sleeper` before the first publish run.

- Are any of the 10 Candidate products seasonal (e.g. a Christmas-themed product)? If so, they should be scheduled ahead of others to hit peak UK buyer interest — worth checking product names before ordering the batch.
- Has the Amazon Associates account generated any qualifying clicks yet? task_brief:
Task: Analyse Picky Products and produce a prioritised improvement plan. Picky Products is a Pinterest-focused affiliate marketing side project targeting UK sleep product buyers. It is largely autonomous — AI agents handle content selection, image generation, and daily pin publishing. The owner's goal is to earn the first £ of affiliate revenue with under 30 minutes of manual work per week. Known gaps: pin image quality feels generic, and there is no visibility into Pinterest performance. New channels are open for analysis if they have a clear revenue advantage.

Project stack:
- Notion: Products DB and Distribution DB (source of truth)
- Python + Pillow: pin image generation (1000x1500px, white background, rounded pill caption)
- Pinterest API via Make webhook: daily publisher posts 1 pin/day
- Amazon Associates UK: monetisation (PA API pending at 3 qualifying sales)
- GitHub Pages: hosts pin images for the Make webhook

Current state:
- 10 products in Candidate status (awaiting /process-product)
- 2 products in active publishing queue (SKYNY Cooling Pillow, Slumberdown British Wool Pillow)
- 3 previously distributed products (Silentnight Cool Touch Pillow, LunaCore Hybrid Pillow, MUSICOZY Bluetooth Sleep Mask)
- Personas: Hot Sleepers, Light Sleepers, Anxious/Insomniac Sleepers (3 per product)
- 9 pins per product: 3 angles × 3 variants (clean label + 2 hook variants)
- Publishing cadence: 1 pin/day via daily cron

Content workflow:
1. /process-product: looks up product in Notion, writes copy for 9 pins, creates Distribution DB records, writes hooks.json and schedule_meta.json
2. Add product.jpg manually (from Amazon listing)
3. /generate-pins: runs generate_pins.py, copies to GitHub Pages, updates Notion status
4. publish_due_pins.py: daily publisher sends due pins to Make → Pinterest

Pin image template:
- White background
- Large product image centred
- Rounded pill at bottom with bold dark text (hook or angle label)
- No A/B testing of templates currently

Analyse and produce a prioritised improvement plan covering:
1. Pin image quality and visual differentiation
2. Pinterest performance visibility (analytics, feedback loops)
3. Monetisation acceleration (path to first £, PA API, link optimisation)
4. Content pipeline throughput (processing 10 waiting Candidates faster)
5. New channel opportunities if clearly higher ROI than Pinterest
6. Operational efficiency (reducing the under-30-min weekly budget further)

Prioritise recommendations by revenue impact and implementation effort. Flag any quick wins (high impact, low effort) explicitly.
Constraints:
- Owner has under 30 minutes per week for manual work — all recommendations must account for this
- Solo operator — no team, no paid tools budget beyond what's already in use
- UK market only — Amazon UK, UK buyer personas, British English throughout
- Pinterest is primary channel — do not recommend abandoning it
- Notion, Python, and Claude Code are available for automation
- No paid advertising — organic growth only
